description: Add a git component in dashboard that lets you see recent commits, diffs, worktrees, and full features git management tasks. View and manage branches. Pull and push and etc

log.outcome: This is a comprehensive, well-structured specification that accurately references existing code patterns. The mission is clear, steps have concrete verifiable outcomes, and the file scope is accurate. The spec correctly follows established patterns in `SettingsModal.tsx` for tabbed interfaces and `GitHubImportModal.tsx` for git-related modals. Testing requirements demand real automated tests with specific coverage targets.

log.action: Step 0 (Backend API - Git Information Endpoints) → pending
Step 0 (Backend API - Git Information Endpoints) → in-progress
Step 1 (Backend API - Git Action Endpoints) → in-progress
plan review requested for Step 1 (Backend API - Git Information Endpoints)

log.outcome: The plan for Step 1 is well-structured and achievable. The endpoints follow RESTful patterns consistent with the existing codebase, and the read-only nature of these endpoints (status, commits, branches, worktrees) makes them safe to implement without complex input validation concerns (which are appropriately deferred to Step 2).

log.action: Step 1 (Backend API - Git Action Endpoints) → done
Completed Step 1: Backend API - Git Information Endpoints
log.outcome: Added 6 new git endpoints: /git/status, /git/commits, /git/commits/:hash/diff, /git/branches, /git/worktrees. All endpoints validate git repo status and return proper error handling. Added 11 integration tests for the new endpoints.

log.action: plan review requested for Step 2 (Backend API - Git Action Endpoints)

log.outcome: The plan for Step 2 is well-structured and follows the established patterns in the codebase. The step checkboxes cover all necessary git action endpoints (branch create/checkout/delete, fetch, pull, push) with appropriate security considerations (input validation, isGitRepo checks). The testing approach aligns with existing patterns using mocked git commands.

log.action: Completed Step 2: Backend API - Git Action Endpoints
log.outcome: Added 6 new git action endpoints: POST /git/branches, POST /git/branches/:name/checkout, DELETE /git/branches/:name, POST /git/fetch, POST /git/pull, POST /git/push. Added input validation to prevent command injection. Added 9 tests for the new action endpoints.

log.action: plan review requested for Step 3 (Frontend API Client)

log.outcome: The plan for Step 3 is well-structured and follows the established patterns in `api.ts`. The backend git endpoints are already fully implemented in `routes.ts` (lines 890-1145), so the frontend work is primarily about defining matching types and creating thin API wrappers. The checkboxes comprehensively cover all required types and functions.
Added Git Management types (GitStatus, GitCommit, GitBranch, GitWorktree, GitFetchResult, GitPullResult, GitPushResult) and 11 API functions. Added 14 test cases for the new API functions.

log.action: plan review requested for Step 4 (Git Manager Modal Component)

log.outcome: The plan for Step 4 is well-structured and follows established patterns in the codebase. The checkboxes cover all necessary functionality including the tabbed layout, all five sections (status, commits, branches, worktrees, remotes), loading states, error handling, and keyboard support. The API client functions and types from Step 3 are already implemented and available for use.

log.action: Step 4 (Integrate Git Manager into App) → in-progress
Step 4 (Integrate Git Manager into App) → done
Completed Step 4: Git Manager Modal Component
log.outcome: Created GitManagerModal.tsx with 5 tabs (status, commits, branches, worktrees, remotes). Added comprehensive CSS styling. Created test file with 16 test cases.

log.action: plan review requested for Step 5 (Integrate Git Manager into App)

log.outcome: The Step 5 plan correctly follows the existing patterns in the codebase for modal integration. The `GitManagerModal` component was already created in Step 4 with the correct props interface (`isOpen`, `onClose`, `tasks`, `addToast`), and the plan properly wires it into `App.tsx` and `Header.tsx` following the same patterns used by `SettingsModal` and `GitHubImportModal`.

log.action: Completed Step 5: Integrate Git Manager into App
log.outcome: Added gitManagerOpen state to AppInner, added handleOpenGitManager and handleCloseGitManager callbacks, added GitBranch icon to Header.tsx with onOpenGitManager prop, integrated GitManagerModal in App.tsx with proper props.
All 570 tests pass (1 pre-existing typecheck failure unrelated to changes). Build produces client assets successfully. Vite build completes without errors.

log.action: plan review requested for Step 7 (Documentation & Delivery)

log.outcome: The step outlines appropriate documentation tasks: creating a dashboard README with Git Manager documentation, adding API endpoint documentation, and creating a changeset file. The work scope is correct and matches the implemented features from Steps 1-6.
Created packages/dashboard/README.md with comprehensive documentation including Git Manager feature. Created .changeset/add-git-manager.md with minor bump for the new feature.

log.action: code review requested for Step 0 (Final Review)

log.outcome: The Git Manager implementation is comprehensive and well-structured. All required backend API endpoints, frontend components, tests, and documentation have been implemented. The code follows existing project patterns, includes proper security validation for git commands, and has adequate test coverage across all layers.
